Joined the Ricovrati in 1689. On the 16th of June 1691, there was a public meeting which was attended by several women, including Lucrezia Capello Gradenigo, the spouse of the `podesta` `, Maria Zorzi Loredana, and several other women from Padua and from aborad. In this context, Obizzi delivered the following oration: `Se fusse piu` lodevole il costume d`escluder le donne dal governo come i Romani, o pure d`ammetterle come i Germani`. Apparently, the topic raised a heated debate. Marco Andrea Zambelli agreed with the first point, while Matteo Forzadura stood for the second option. A couple of years later, in the public meeting which took place in Mantova Benavides family garden, Obizzi had a conversation with Vitaliano Mussato on the following problem: se porti piu` vantaggio nel cuor delle donne a chi le serve il sofferir le loro amorose ingiurie o il rissentirsene.

A letter of his from Padua, addressed to Camillo Vizzani in Rome, dated 22 July 1566, is in the Ranuzzi MSS Ph 12670 at the Harry Ransom Center. The letter mentions Scipione Gonzaga and two Academies, presumably the Eterei Academy in Padua, and the Oziosi academy in Bologna, to which all three apparently belonged: `... non restero` di dargli di V. S. quella notizia che io debbo, et pigliero` fra l`altre occasione dell`Accademia nostra, essendone egli protettore...`. Varoli wishes that when Gonzaga will be in Bologna `... gli fosse fatto dalla nostra Academia una sessione pubblica nella quale si dicessero le sue lode et se gli facesse dono della Academia...` In particular, Varoli asks Vezzani whether he would be willing to use for such occasion a canzone he had previously composed.
